name: Riley
observedShift: Riley is in week two and is moving quickly through codebase orientation, but he has mostly learned from docs and async notes rather than direct team contact. I want to check whether he feels clear on ownership boundaries, incident expectations, and where to ask for context before making architecture assumptions. There is a risk he looks productive while building an internal map that differs from how the team actually operates. This check-in should identify missing context early and convert it into concrete onboarding support.

focusPoints.label: Ownership boundaries and what “good” means here.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he feels clear on ownership boundaries and expectations for his level, especially around what he should drive vs what he should escalate or coordinate.

focusPoints.label: Where to ask for context before assumptions.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he knows the right places to get team-specific context so he doesn’t build an internal map that differs from how the team actually operates.

focusPoints.label: Incident expectations and escalation norms.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he understands incident expectations and who he should loop in early, so he can act correctly under pressure rather than relying on docs.

focusPoints.label: Concrete onboarding support you can add now.
focusPoints.reason: What he would want more of from you during onboarding—fast feedback loops, clearer decision context, and explicit introductions—so missing context gets filled early instead of later.

issues: listenFor may lack observable behavioural cue: "if they describe ownership boundaries in ways that differ fr…"
